seoTitle: Switch to Classic View in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Miss the old layout? Here's how to revert to Classic View in QuickBooks Online after an interface update, plus how to make it stick.
intro: When QuickBooks Online rolls out a new interface, familiar menus can suddenly feel out of reach. This guide shows how to switch back to the Classic View so you can keep working without hunting for relocated tools.

steps.heading: Open the settings menu
steps.body: Click the gear icon in the top right corner of your QuickBooks Online dashboard to open the settings dropdown.

steps.heading: Find the view switcher
steps.body: Under the Your Company column, look for a link labeled Switch to Classic View or Switch to Accountant View.

steps.heading: Confirm the switch
steps.body: Click the switch link, optionally leave feedback on why you're reverting, and confirm to trigger the layout change.

steps.heading: Verify the classic layout
steps.body: Check that your left navigation sidebar and transaction hubs now match the familiar legacy menu structure.

steps.heading: Lock in your preference
steps.body: Review your settings to make sure QuickBooks remembers your choice so it defaults to Classic View the next time you log in.

faq.question: Will switching to Classic View delete any data?
faq.answer: No, changing your view is purely a layout preference and does not affect your transactions or account data.

faq.question: Can I switch back to the new view later?
faq.answer: Yes, the same settings menu lets you toggle back to the updated interface whenever you choose.

faq.question: Why can't I find the Classic View option?
faq.answer: It's usually located under the gear icon's Your Company section; if it's still missing, your account type or QuickBooks version may not offer it.

faq.question: Does Classic View affect reports or transaction hubs?
faq.answer: It mainly changes the navigation layout, but you may notice differences in how invoices, banking, and reports are organized on screen.
